title: Build guarded paper execution bridge from mlb_model recommendations to sports_bot_v2
objective: Create a narrow bridge module that reads approved mlb_model shadow recommendations, applies strict gating, and creates paper trades in sports_bot_v2. Paper mode only. No real money execution. No architecture redesign.
paper_only_notice: THIS IS PAPER MODE ONLY. No real money is involved. No live order placement. No connection to any exchange, broker, or betting platform. The bridge reads a local log file and writes to a local SQLite paper trade database only.
do_not_touch: execution logic beyond calling existing paper_exec
inputs: C:\Users\johnny\Desktop\mlb_model\logs\shadow_recommendations.jsonl — read the last N entries to find fresh recommendations
C:\Users\johnny\Desktop\sports_bot_v2\core\paper_exec.py — read to understand how to create a paper position (do not modify)
C:\Users\johnny\Desktop\sports_bot_v2\core\db.py — read to understand how open positions are queried (do not modify)
C:\Users\johnny\Desktop\sports_bot_v2\runtime\state.json — read to understand current open positions
architecture: model_bridge.py is a pure gating module. It reads the shadow log, applies all gates, and returns a list of approved paper trade intents.
bot_core.py gets a minimal addition: after its existing discovery loop, call model_bridge.get_approved_intents(), then for each approved intent call the existing paper_exec machinery.
Do not replace or bypass any existing bot_core logic. The bridge is additive only.
The shadow log is read as a flat file — no import of mlb_model code.
log_deduplication_rules: The shadow log is an audit/history source, not a clean execution feed. It may contain repeated recommendations for the same slug across many loops.
Before applying any gates, reduce the log to the LATEST entry per market_slug only. Use feature_timestamp or ts to determine recency.
Ignore all entries where action=NO_TRADE — these are never execution candidates.
After per-slug deduplication, the working set must contain at most one entry per slug.
This deduplication happens inside model_bridge.py before gate evaluation — not after.
required_gates: model_version must match a known approved version string (e.g. mlb_winprob_v1_lgbm)
recommendation age must be below MAX_REC_AGE_SECONDS (suggested: 120s)
market_type must be moneyline — reject spreads, totals, props, NRFI
event date in slug must match today's date
is_live must be True in the recommendation payload
side must be explicit: BUY_YES or BUY_NO — never NONE or ambiguous
no existing open paper position for this event slug already in DB
no duplicate: only one approved intent per event slug per loop
kill_switch: Add ENABLE_MODEL_BRIDGE = False at the top of model_bridge.py as a hard config constant.
If ENABLE_MODEL_BRIDGE is False, get_approved_intents() returns an empty list immediately and logs: BRIDGE DISABLED — set ENABLE_MODEL_BRIDGE=True to activate.
Default must be False. The worker must not set it to True in the submitted patch.
The user will manually flip it to True after verifying the bridge logic in a test run.
paper_trade_behavior: If all gates pass, call the existing paper_exec machinery to open a paper position
Position must be tagged with source='model_bridge' — this exact string, used consistently
Sizing: use the same default sizing already in paper_exec — do not invent new sizing logic
One position per game maximum — enforced by gate above
The paper trade appears in the existing DB and dashboard naturally via existing infrastructure
deliverables: model_bridge.py — new module with all gating logic and clear constants at the top
minimal diff to bot_core.py — only the bridge call and import, nothing else
summary of all gates and their threshold values used
expected log output showing a gate pass and a gate rejection
acceptance_criteria: model_bridge.py exists with all required gates implemented
ENABLE_MODEL_BRIDGE = False is present at top of model_bridge.py and defaults to False in submitted patch
get_approved_intents() returns empty list and logs BRIDGE DISABLED when ENABLE_MODEL_BRIDGE is False
log deduplication: only latest entry per slug is evaluated, NO_TRADE entries are discarded before gating
bot_core.py calls model_bridge and passes approved intents to paper_exec
moneyline-only gate enforced — test that a spread recommendation is rejected
same-day date gate enforced — test that a future-dated recommendation is rejected
staleness gates enforced — rec age, game state age, book age all checked
duplicate position gate enforced — second intent for same slug is rejected
paper trade created when all gates pass — position appears in state.json or DB
source label 'model_bridge' present on created position (exact string)
no changes to paper_exec.py, risk.py, signal_base.py
no real execution path exists — paper only
verification: Inspect logs for: BRIDGE GATE PASS and BRIDGE GATE REJECT entries
Inspect runtime/state.json for any new paper position with source=model-driven
Confirm no changes to paper_exec.py, risk.py, signal_base.py
rollback: Delete core/model_bridge.py and revert bot_core.py only. No other files were modified.
